description:
You are an edge-computing engineer preparing an OTA (over-the-air) upgrade rollout for a fleet of IoT gateways.
    A staged directory tree already exists in your home folder at:

        /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.0/
        /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.1/
        /home/user/edge_app/current   (symbolic link that still points to v1.2.0)

    Your goal is to promote v1.2.1 to production, keep an explicit reference to the previous release, and document the change in an immutable log file.

    Required end-state (what automated tests will check):
    1. Symbolic link “/home/user/edge_app/current” MUST point to:
           /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.1
    2. A **new** symbolic link “/home/user/edge_app/previous” MUST exist and point to:
           /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.0
         (If the link already exists, update it so it meets this requirement.)
    3. Directory “/home/user/deployment_logs” MUST exist (create it if necessary).
    4. Inside that directory there MUST be a plain-text log file named:
           /home/user/deployment_logs/symlink_update.log
       with EXACTLY the four lines shown below (the automated test performs a byte-for-byte comparison, including newlines):

           TIMESTAMP: 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z
           OLD_CURRENT: /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.0
           NEW_CURRENT: /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.1
           PREVIOUS_LINK: /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.0

       Notes on the required format:
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline after the fourth line.  
       • Do not add extra spaces, blank lines, or carriage returns.  
       • Use the literal timestamp shown above (the CI environment expects that fixed value).

    Operational constraints:
    • Work entirely as the regular “user” account—no root actions.  
    • You can assume the release directories already hold the compiled binaries; you do **not** need to modify their contents.  
    • Completing the task will realistically need between 5 and 10 shell commands (create a directory, manage symlinks, write the log file, optionally list to verify).

    Deliverables the grader will verify:
    • Correct targets of the two symbolic links.  
    • Existence and exact contents of /home/user/deployment_logs/symlink_update.log.  

    Make sure your commands leave the system in the exact state described above so the automated tests pass.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing setup before the agent starts -->
    <initial_state>
        /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.0/ (directory, mode 755)
            └── build.txt  (contents: "v1.2.0 build completed\n")
        /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.1/ (directory, mode 755)
            └── build.txt  (contents: "v1.2.1 build completed\n")
        /home/user/edge_app/current -> /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.0
        # /home/user/edge_app/previous does not exist
        # /home/user/deployment_logs/ directory does not exist
    </initial_state>

    <!-- Required final state for automated grading -->
    <final_state>
        Symlink: /home/user/edge_app/current
            -> /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.1
        Symlink: /home/user/edge_app/previous
            -> /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.0

        Directory: /home/user/deployment_logs (mode 755, owned by user)
        File: /home/user/deployment_logs/symlink_update.log (mode 644)
        Contents (4 lines + trailing newline):
            TIMESTAMP: 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\n
            OLD_CURRENT: /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.0\n
            NEW_CURRENT: /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.1\n
            PREVIOUS_LINK: /home/user/edge_app/releases/v1.2.0\n
    </final_state>
